nodes.summary: blueprint.generated.mk MUST declare SPEC_SCAFFOLD_DEFAULT_TRACK ?= blueprint and use it in the spec-scaffold recipe
blueprint.generated.mk MUST declare BLUEPRINT_UPLIFT_STATUS_SCRIPT ?= scripts/bin/blueprint/uplift_status.sh and use it in the blueprint-uplift-status recipe
Both declarations MUST use ?= so consumer platform.mk can override with := without triggering an override warning
Template file MUST be updated and make blueprint-render-makefile MUST produce the updated generated file
Change MUST be backward compatible — consumers not setting the new variables see identical behavior
Consumer repos that previously produced GNU Make override warnings MUST produce no warnings after fix adoption
make spec-scaffold with no override uses --track blueprint and produces no warning
Consumer setting SPEC_SCAFFOLD_DEFAULT_TRACK := consumer causes --track consumer with no warning
make blueprint-uplift-status with no override calls scripts/bin/blueprint/uplift_status.sh with no warning
Consumer setting BLUEPRINT_UPLIFT_STATUS_SCRIPT to a custom path causes the target to call that path with no warning
test_quality_contracts.py contains assertions verifying both ?= variable declarations in template and generated file
